content.json:
# 🌍 Exfil-by-Volume

Maps external destination IPs parsed from `DestPublicIps` in `NTANetAnalytics`, sized by bytes leaving the network (`bytesOut`). A fat bubble in an unusual region = a large outbound transfer — the data-exfil signal the MDE tables can't show.

> Enriched with `geo_info_from_ip_address()`. Only destinations resolved to a **city + country** are shown. Geo data (MaxMind GeoLite2) is approximate.

## 4️⃣ Exfil-by-Volume — `NTANetAnalytics`

Maps the external **destination** public IP of VNet flows, sized by **bytes leaving your network**. The IP and byte counters are parsed out of the `DestPublicIps` tuple (`IP|flowStarted|flowEnded|allowedInFlows|deniedInFlows|bytesIn|bytesOut`), then geo-enriched.

Bubble **size** = `bytesOut` (data sent). **A fat bubble in an unusual region = a large outbound transfer — the true data-exfil signal the MDE tables can't show.** Drill into any single destination that dominates total egress.

> Filters to `SubType == "FlowLog"` and to destinations resolved to a city + country. Byte counters here are populated, so volume-based sizing is meaningful.

content.title: Outbound volume by destination — bubble size = bytes sent

content.query:
// Companion grid: destinations ranked by total bytes out, with the
// internal source fan-out feeding each (concentration = exfil staging).
NTANetAnalytics
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where SubType == "FlowLog"
| where isnotempty(DestPublicIps)
| extend Parts = split(DestPublicIps, "|")
| extend PublicIp    = tostring(Parts[0]),
         DeniedFlows = tolong(Parts[4]),
         BytesIn     = tolong(Parts[5]),
         BytesOut    = tolong(Parts[6])
| where isnotempty(PublicIp) and BytesOut > 0
| extend geo = geo_info_from_ip_address(PublicIp)
| extend Country = tostring(geo.country),
         City    = tostring(geo.city)
// Only keep destinations the geo DB placed to a city + country.
| where isnotempty(City) and isnotempty(Country)
| summarize BytesOut = sum(BytesOut),
            BytesIn  = sum(BytesIn),
            Sources  = dcount(SrcIp),
            Ports    = make_set(DestPort, 15)
         by PublicIp, Country, City
| extend MB_Out = round(BytesOut / 1048576.0, 1)
| project PublicIp, Country, City, BytesOut, MBOut = round(toreal(BytesOut)/1024, 1),
          GBOut = round(toreal(BytesOut)/1024/1024, 1),
          TBOut = round(toreal(BytesOut)/1024/1024/1024, 1), BytesIn, Sources, Ports
| order by BytesOut desc
content.title: Destination volume breakdown (MB out + source fan-out)
